programmes.show_description: Get your fix of all the best new releases in one handy playlist.
Wesley Joseph on his debut record and ex-Black Midi outfit My New Band Believe and Gretel breakdown their new LPs. Plus, Matt Everitt picks tunes from Squarepusher and Lime Garden.
Soundtrack the early hours with back-to-back music, plus exclusive BBC sessions.
6 Music goes back to the 90s!
6 Music goes back to the 00s!
Chris Hawkins takes you through 30 minutes of remixed, re-versioned and reimagined tracks. Featuring Gorillaz, The Streets, Cassius, Foals and more.
Amy Lamé has all the best new music to get you through to your weekend. Get in touch with your Friday shouts, mentions and requests. WhatsApp 03700 100 600 or text 64046.
Comedians Roisin Conaty and Sara Pascoe join Nick to chat about their new series Zero Stars, in which they visit holiday destinations with particularly baffling online reviews.
Musician, writer, actor Adele Bertei shares her Desert Island Disco.
The weekend is basically here, so Chris is giving YOU permission to start the party early. That's right, let's Down Tools with LOADS of amazing tunes to get us going.
It's Friday, so Emily is out to prove that any song sounds better when it's played on 6 Music today - so let us know what you want to hear.

programmes.show_name: The People's Party with Femi Koleoso
programmes.show_description: The London jungle production duo share their Friday night tracks and stories in the wake of their new EP, Love On The Line with collaborator Layla Sibelle.
Indie bangers from Warpaint and Arctics, plus Nathan chats to Gary Jarman of The Cribs!
Gary Jarman from The Cribs takes over the Indie Forever playlist!

programmes.show_name: The 6 Mix with Mary Anne Hobbs
programmes.show_description: Quiet Husband is the DJ and producer alias of British multidisciplinary artist Richie Culver. A visceral project rooted in the industrial landscapes and rave culture of Hull.
Exploring The Purple One's musical DNA in the rave with an hour of Prince-inspired electronic music.
6 Music takes you to the dark side with the finest goth, electronic and dark pop music, featuring The Cramps, Kathryn Joseph, Gesaffelstein, Whipping Boy, aya and more.
The jazz keyboardist and producer selects the first hour of this focus mix. Featuring tracks from Slum Village, Hiatus Kaiyote, Dilla, Madlib and more.
Inspired by summer by the sea, Christina Vantzou selects two hours of ambient tracks to deepen your concentration, including music by Holland Andrews, Gail Loughton and Coby Sey .
Need to slow things down? Afrodeutsche brings the mellow Saturday flow you need to really hit that deeply relaxing vibe.
Mark and Stuart present a feast of great music and actor James McAvoy shares his 'First, Last and Everything' and chats about his latest film and directorial debut.
Beth's back for round two with musical gems new and old, plus a Caffeine Kick at 11!
Zakia sits in with alternative RnB, global club sounds and leftfield electronica.
Joining the musical dots, soul, hip-hop, house, Afro, Latin, electronica, jazz and beyond.

programmes.show_name: The Stuart Maconie Funk and Soul Show
programmes.show_description: Andy Platts from Mamas Gun is Stuart's guest, talking about the new album DIG!.
Don breaks format with an essential selection of reggae covers. Expect bass-heavy takes on the likes of The Beatles, Bronski Beat, Ray Charles, Dusty Springfield and Blur.
SHERELLE lights up your Saturday night with some high-NRG drum and bass.
